speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Welcome, my lord, to this brave town of York.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Yonder’s the head of that arch-enemy

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That sought to be encompassed with your crown.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Doth not the object cheer your heart, my lord?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ay, as the rocks cheer them that fear their wrack!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To see this sight, it irks my very soul.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Withhold revenge, dear God! ’Tis not my fault,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Nor wittingly have I infringed my vow.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: My gracious liege, this too much lenity

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And harmful pity must be laid aside.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: To whom do lions cast their gentle looks?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Not to the beast that would usurp their den.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Whose hand is that the forest bear doth lick?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Not his that spoils her young before her face.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Who scapes the lurking serpent’s mortal sting?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Not he that sets his foot upon her back.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: The smallest worm will turn, being trodden on,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And doves will peck in safeguard of their brood.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Ambitious York did level at thy crown,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Thou smiling while he knit his angry brows.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: He, but a duke, would have his son a king

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And raise his issue like a loving sire;

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Thou being a king, blest with a goodly son,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Didst yield consent to disinherit him,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Which argued thee a most unloving father.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Unreasonable creatures feed their young;

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And though man’s face be fearful to their eyes,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Yet in protection of their tender ones,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Who hath not seen them, even with those wings

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Which sometime they have used with fearful flight,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Make war with him that climbed unto their nest,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Offering their own lives in their young’s defense?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: For shame, my liege, make them your precedent.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Were it not pity that this goodly boy

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Should lose his birthright by his father’s fault,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And long hereafter say unto his child

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: “What my great-grandfather and grandsire got,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: My careless father fondly gave away”?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Ah, what a shame were this! Look on the boy,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And let his manly face, which promiseth

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Successful fortune, steel thy melting heart

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: To hold thine own and leave thine own with him.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Full well hath Clifford played the orator,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Inferring arguments of mighty force.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But, Clifford, tell me, didst thou never hear

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That things ill got had ever bad success?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And happy always was it for that son

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whose father for his hoarding went to hell?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I’ll leave my son my virtuous deeds behind,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And would my father had left me no more;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For all the rest is held at such a rate

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: As brings a thousandfold more care to keep

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Than in possession any jot of pleasure.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ah, cousin York, would thy best friends did know

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How it doth grieve me that thy head is here.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: My lord, cheer up your spirits; our foes are nigh,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And this soft courage makes your followers faint.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: You promised knighthood to our forward son.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Unsheathe your sword and dub him presently.—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Edward, kneel down.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Edward Plantagenet, arise a knight,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And learn this lesson: draw thy sword in right.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: My gracious father, by your kingly leave,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: I’ll draw it as apparent to the crown

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: And in that quarrel use it to the death.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Why, that is spoken like a toward prince.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Royal commanders, be in readiness,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: For with a band of thirty thousand men

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Comes Warwick backing of the Duke of York,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And in the towns as they do march along

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Proclaims him king, and many fly to him.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Deraign your battle, for they are at hand.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: I would your Highness would depart the field.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: The Queen hath best success when you are absent.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Ay, good my lord, and leave us to our fortune.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why, that’s my fortune too; therefore I’ll stay.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Be it with resolution, then, to fight.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: My royal father, cheer these noble lords

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: And hearten those that fight in your defense.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: Unsheathe your sword, good father; cry “Saint George!”

speaker: EDWARD
text: Now, perjured Henry, wilt thou kneel for grace

speaker: EDWARD
text: And set thy diadem upon my head,

speaker: EDWARD
text: Or bide the mortal fortune of the field?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Go rate thy minions, proud insulting boy.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Becomes it thee to be thus bold in terms

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Before thy sovereign and thy lawful king?

speaker: EDWARD
text: I am his king, and he should bow his knee.

speaker: EDWARD
text: I was adopted heir by his consent.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Since when, his oath is broke; for, as I hear,

speaker: EDWARD
text: You that are king, though he do wear the crown,

speaker: EDWARD
text: Have caused him, by new act of Parliament,

speaker: EDWARD
text: To blot out me and put his own son in.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And reason too:

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Who should succeed the father but the son?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Are you there, butcher? O, I cannot speak!

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Ay, crookback, here I stand to answer thee,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Or any he, the proudest of thy sort.

speaker: RICHARD
text: ’Twas you that killed young Rutland, was it not?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Ay, and old York, and yet not satisfied.

speaker: RICHARD
text: For God’s sake, lords, give signal to the fight!

speaker: WARWICK
text: What sayst thou, Henry? Wilt thou yield the crown?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Why, how now, long-tongued Warwick, dare you speak?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: When you and I met at Saint Albans last,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Your legs did better service than your hands.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Then ’twas my turn to fly, and now ’tis thine.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: You said so much before, and yet you fled.

speaker: WARWICK
text: ’Twas not your valor, Clifford, drove me thence.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: No, nor your manhood that durst make you stay.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Northumberland, I hold thee reverently.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Break off the parley, for scarce I can refrain

speaker: RICHARD
text: The execution of my big-swoll’n heart

speaker: RICHARD
text: Upon that Clifford, that cruel child-killer.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: I slew thy father; call’st thou him a child?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ay, like a dastard and a treacherous coward,

speaker: RICHARD
text: As thou didst kill our tender brother Rutland.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But ere sunset I’ll make thee curse the deed.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Have done with words, my lords, and hear me speak.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Defy them, then, or else hold close thy lips.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I prithee, give no limits to my tongue.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I am a king and privileged to speak.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: My liege, the wound that bred this meeting here

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Cannot be cured by words; therefore, be still.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then, executioner, unsheathe thy sword.

speaker: RICHARD
text: By Him that made us all, I am resolved

speaker: RICHARD
text: That Clifford’s manhood lies upon his tongue.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Say, Henry, shall I have my right or no?

speaker: EDWARD
text: A thousand men have broke their fasts today

speaker: EDWARD
text: That ne’er shall dine unless thou yield the crown.

speaker: WARWICK
text: If thou deny, their blood upon thy head,

speaker: WARWICK
text: For York in justice puts his armor on.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: If that be right which Warwick says is right,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: There is no wrong, but everything is right.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Whoever got thee, there thy mother stands,

speaker: RICHARD
text: For well I wot thou hast thy mother’s tongue.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But thou art neither like thy sire nor dam,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But like a foul misshapen stigmatic,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Marked by the Destinies to be avoided,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: As venom toads or lizards’ dreadful stings.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Iron of Naples, hid with English gilt,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Whose father bears the title of a king,

speaker: RICHARD
text: As if a channel should be called the sea,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Sham’st thou not, knowing whence thou art extraught,

speaker: RICHARD
text: To let thy tongue detect thy baseborn heart?

speaker: EDWARD
text: A wisp of straw were worth a thousand crowns

speaker: EDWARD
text: To make this shameless callet know herself.—

speaker: EDWARD
text: Helen of Greece was fairer far than thou,

speaker: EDWARD
text: Although thy husband may be Menelaus;

speaker: EDWARD
text: And ne’er was Agamemnon’s brother wronged

speaker: EDWARD
text: By that false woman as this king by thee.

speaker: EDWARD
text: His father reveled in the heart of France,

speaker: EDWARD
text: And tamed the King, and made the Dauphin stoop;

speaker: EDWARD
text: And had he matched according to his state,

speaker: EDWARD
text: He might have kept that glory to this day.

speaker: EDWARD
text: But when he took a beggar to his bed

speaker: EDWARD
text: And graced thy poor sire with his bridal day,

speaker: EDWARD
text: Even then that sunshine brewed a shower for him

speaker: EDWARD
text: That washed his father’s fortunes forth of France

speaker: EDWARD
text: And heaped sedition on his crown at home.

speaker: EDWARD
text: For what hath broached this tumult but thy pride?

speaker: EDWARD
text: Hadst thou been meek, our title still had slept,

speaker: EDWARD
text: And we, in pity of the gentle king,

speaker: EDWARD
text: Had slipped our claim until another age.

speaker: GEORGE
text: But when we saw our sunshine made thy spring,

speaker: GEORGE
text: And that thy summer bred us no increase,

speaker: GEORGE
text: We set the axe to thy usurping root;

speaker: GEORGE
text: And though the edge hath something hit ourselves,

speaker: GEORGE
text: Yet know thou, since we have begun to strike,

speaker: GEORGE
text: We’ll never leave till we have hewn thee down

speaker: GEORGE
text: Or bathed thy growing with our heated bloods.

speaker: EDWARD
text: And in this resolution, I defy thee,

speaker: EDWARD
text: Not willing any longer conference,

speaker: EDWARD
text: Since thou denied’st the gentle king to speak.—

speaker: EDWARD
text: Sound, trumpets! Let our bloody colors wave;

speaker: EDWARD
text: And either victory or else a grave!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Stay, Edward!

speaker: EDWARD
text: No, wrangling woman, we’ll no longer stay.

speaker: EDWARD
text: These words will cost ten thousand lives this day.
